title: What is the purpose of @Configuration and @Bean annotations in Spring?

answers.junior: @Configuration marks a class for Spring to use as a source of bean definitions. @Bean defines a method that returns a bean.
answers.middle: @Configuration classes group bean definitions. @Bean methods create and configure beans. Use them for complex setup or third-party integration.
answers.senior: @Configuration enables Java-based configuration. @Bean methods allow custom bean creation logic. Use for integrating libraries or advanced setup. Annotation-based config is preferred for maintainability.
answers.tricky: @Configuration classes are processed by Spring to register beans. @Bean methods can use parameters for dependency injection. Misconfiguring @Bean methods or forgetting @Configuration can cause missing beans or errors.
